managers.name: Mauricio Pochettino
importance: Managed Messi during his debut season at PSG.
Integrated Messi into the attacking trio with Neymar and Mbappé.

managers.name: Christophe Galtier
importance: Oversaw Messi's most productive season at PSG.
Utilized Messi primarily as a deep-lying playmaker and creator.

shirt_history.reason: The iconic No.10 shirt was already worn by Neymar, so Messi chose No.30—the number he wore when making his senior debut at Barcelona.

playing_roles.description: Started from the right flank but frequently drifted into central areas to create chances and combine with Neymar and Mbappé.
Played close to Mbappé, linking midfield and attack while creating space through movement and intelligent positioning.
Dropped deeper to orchestrate attacks, dictate tempo and provide key passes rather than acting solely as a goalscorer.
Became PSG's primary creator, leading Ligue 1 in assists and consistently initiating attacking moves.

legacy.status: One of the greatest players to represent Paris Saint-Germain
considered: One of the greatest footballers of all time
legacy.description: Although his stay lasted only two seasons, Messi helped Paris Saint-Germain win back-to-back Ligue 1 titles and a Trophée des Champions. He formed the celebrated MNM attacking trio alongside Neymar and Kylian Mbappé, evolved into one of Europe's finest playmakers, led Ligue 1 in assists during the 2022-23 season, won the FIFA World Cup while representing PSG, and became the first player in history to win the Ballon d'Or while playing for a French club since George Weah in 1995.

individual_awards: IFFHS World's Best International Goal Scorer (2022)
major_events: Signed for Paris Saint-Germain after leaving Barcelona.
Made his PSG debut against Reims.
Scored his first PSG goal against Manchester City in the UEFA Champions League.
Won his first Ligue 1 title with PSG.
Adapted to a new league after spending his entire senior career at Barcelona.
Formed the 'MNM' attacking trio with Neymar and Kylian Mbappé.
Registered the most assists for PSG during the league season.
Reached the UEFA Champions League Round of 16 before elimination by Real Madrid.

iconic_matches.description: Scored his first official goal for PSG with a trademark left-foot finish after combining with Kylian Mbappé.
Made his official debut for Paris Saint-Germain after replacing Neymar in the second half.
Scored a spectacular long-range goal as PSG secured the Ligue 1 title.

records_broken: First Argentine player to score for PSG in the UEFA Champions League.
Won a league title in a second European country.

individual_awards: UNFP Ligue 1 Team of the Year
major_events: Enjoyed his best season for Paris Saint-Germain.
Won the Trophée des Champions to begin the season.
Played a deeper creative role under Christophe Galtier.
Scored the 700th club goal of his professional career.
Won the 2022 FIFA World Cup with Argentina while representing PSG.
Won his second consecutive Ligue 1 title.
Played his final match for PSG before leaving as a free agent.

iconic_matches.description: Produced a masterclass with a goal and two assists, highlighting his improved form.
Scored a brilliant volley and provided another outstanding all-round performance.
Scored his 700th club career goal and helped PSG secure a crucial victory.
Scored the goal that mathematically secured PSG's 11th Ligue 1 title.
Played his final match for Paris Saint-Germain before departing at the end of the season.

records_broken: Became the Ligue 1 assist leader for the 2022-23 season.
Won the FIFA World Cup while representing Paris Saint-Germain.
Helped PSG win a record 11th French league title.

departure.reason: Contract expired and was not renewed.

club_records: First Ballon d'Or winner to play for Paris Saint-Germain.
First FIFA World Cup winner while representing Paris Saint-Germain.
Reached 700 club career goals while playing for PSG.
One of only a few PSG players to record 20+ goals and 20+ assists in a single season across all competitions.
Led Ligue 1 in assists during the 2022-23 season.
Part of PSG's iconic 'MNM' attacking trio alongside Neymar Jr. and Kylian Mbappé.
Contributed 67 goal involvements (32 goals and 35 assists) in 75 official appearances.

major_milestones.event: Signed for Paris Saint-Germain after leaving FC Barcelona.
Scored first PSG goal against Manchester City in the UEFA Champions League.
Won first Ligue 1 title with PSG.
Won the FIFA World Cup while representing Paris Saint-Germain.
Finished as Ligue 1's top assist provider.
Played final match for Paris Saint-Germain.
Left Paris Saint-Germain as a free agent.

timeline.event: Signed a two-year contract with Paris Saint-Germain.
Officially unveiled at Parc des Princes.
Made official PSG debut against Reims.
Scored first PSG goal against Manchester City in the UEFA Champions League.
Scored first Ligue 1 goal for PSG against Nantes.
Won first Ligue 1 title with Paris Saint-Germain.
Won the FIFA World Cup with Argentina while representing PSG.
Scored his 700th club career goal against Marseille.
Scored against Strasbourg as PSG secured the Ligue 1 title.
Played his final match for Paris Saint-Germain against Clermont.
Officially departed Paris Saint-Germain following the expiration of his contract.
